The fact that the checking account balance is negative due to it acting as a significant predictor of default, because it acts as a significant predictor of default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[1]
The fact that the loan duration is extended due to extended tenors acting as a significant positive driver of default, because extended tenors act as a significant positive driver of default, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[2]
The fact that there is no checking account due to the absence of a checking account being noted in credit card default prediction, because the absence of a checking account is noted in credit card default prediction, is known in the literature as a signal that increases credit risk, and the model also identifies it as a factor that increases risk.[3]
Overall, this applicant is likely to have a high risk of default.
